questions.prompt: In a Page Object Model framework, what does a Page Object hide from the test?
options: The assertions
The locators and low-level Selenium calls, exposing high-level actions instead
The test report
The TestNG annotations
questions.explanation: A Page Object exposes actions like loginAs(user, pass) and keeps the locators and raw Selenium calls inside. When a locator changes, you edit the page object, not every test.

questions.prompt: Which tools form the spine of the capstone framework?
options: Jenkins, Cucumber, and Ruby
Page Objects, TestNG tests, Maven for build and dependencies, and a reporter
Only a single long main() method
A spreadsheet of manual steps
questions.explanation: The capstone assembles Page Objects (actions), TestNG test classes (Arrange-Act-Assert), Maven (build and dependencies), and a reporter. Each layer has one job.

questions.prompt: On SauceDemo, which sequence is the capstone end-to-end flow?
options: Open the page and immediately assert the title
Log in, add an item to the cart, check out, fill the form, finish, and assert the confirmation
Log in and log out repeatedly
Submit the contact form only
questions.explanation: The capstone exercises login, cart, checkout form, order completion, and a final assertion on the confirmation text, covering navigation, forms, and verification end to end.

questions.prompt: Why should the checkout test end on a single clear assertion?
options: TestNG crashes with more than one assertion
So a failure points straight at one expected outcome, making the result a diagnosis rather than a mystery
To make the test run in parallel
Assertions slow the browser down
questions.explanation: Focusing the test on one assertion (the order confirmation) means a red result tells you exactly what broke, the same one-behaviour principle from the structure lesson.

questions.prompt: Which best describes 'done' for the capstone framework?
options: It compiles on your laptop and that is enough
Tests pass headless in CI, locators are stable, tests are independent and clearly named, failures produce a report with a screenshot, and a README explains how to run it
It has the most lines of code possible
It runs only when you watch it manually
questions.explanation: Done means the framework runs unattended and headless in CI, uses stable locators, keeps tests independent and well named, reports failures with screenshots, and documents how to run it.
